relations: Coffee's origins can be traced back to the Ethiopian Plateau
Kaldi discovered the stimulating effects of coffee beans, leading to its widespread popularity
After its discovery, coffee was cultivated and traded extensively in the Arabian Peninsula
Qahveh Khaneh were coffee houses in the Middle East where coffee was consumed socially
Coffee's popularity spread to Europe in the 17th century, becoming a central part of social life
Voltaire was known to frequent coffee houses, indicating his participation in the coffee culture of the Enlightenment period
Rousseau was known to frequent coffee houses, indicating his participation in the coffee culture of the Enlightenment period
Coffee played a pivotal role during the Enlightenment Period, facilitating the exchange of ideas among thinkers and writers
Starbucks has played a significant role in transforming coffee into a global phenomenon
The global phenomenon of coffee has led to the emergence of a distinct coffee culture
Coffee's status as a global phenomenon has significant implications for coffee production industries worldwide
Coffee's cultivation has environmental implications such as deforestation, water usage, and biodiversity loss, affecting its own industry
Farmers are responsible for the cultivation of coffee beans, directly impacting the coffee trade and industry
Consumers' demand for ethically sourced and sustainably produced coffee influences the coffee industry's practices
Penny Universities were coffee houses in London, a part of Europe's coffee house culture
Cafe Culture in Paris was a significant event in Europe's social history, influenced by coffee
Coffee production is directly linked to the global coffee trade, impacting economies on both local and global levels
Baristas serve coffee directly to consumers, facilitating the final step in the coffee supply chain
